scope: API market data / market price anchoring in Claude prompt
when_shown: BEFORE research findings — runner block with market_prob_pct appears in user_prompt before key_facts block
prompt_structure: 2. Runners with market implied probabilities (e.g. '- Ribeiro/Zeballos: 62.1% (market implied)')

anchoring_evidence.exact_match_case: Fenerbahce SK v Besiktas — market=75.19%, claude=75.19%, contrary_evidence=None
anchoring_evidence.correlation: LOW overall (avg 18.5pp diff), but 12% of signals show suspected anchoring (<5pp diff)
anchoring_evidence.interpretation: Claude is NOT systematically anchoring — average 18.5pp divergence shows genuine independent assessment. However, 12% of signals (15/128) show <5pp diff, consistent with anchoring when research is weak. One exact match confirmed (Fenerbahce 75.19%). The bigger risk is NOT tight anchoring but the OPPOSITE: when research is absent (junk sources), Claude generates EXTREME outputs (5%, 95%, 100%) rather than staying near the market. This is more dangerous than anchoring.

wang_transform_anchoring_risk.explanation: Wang Transform always compresses market risk premium, generating 3-7pp of structural positive edge at any probability level (e.g. Wang(50%) = 42.7%, so edge = 50% - 42.7% = +7.3pp). If Claude anchors to market_prob, this phantom edge can push signals above the 10pp threshold.

confirmed_false_signals.description: 17 signals where Claude was only 3-7pp above market (likely low-confidence/anchored), but Wang inflated into 10pp+ edge. Examples: Galan (market 41.3%, claude 47.0%, final edge +12.6pp), Djere (market 42.0%, claude 46.0%, edge +11.0pp), Scheffler golf (market 54.4%, claude 59.0%, edge +11.9pp).

wang_transform_anchoring_risk.severity: MEDIUM — 17 phantom signals in history, not systematic but structurally recurring

prior_history_injection.what_it_was: MOD-123: per-runner past bet results injected into research brief as additional key_facts. Dead functions _build_prior_index, _prior_performance_fact, _norm_last in researcher.py + analyze_research.py.
prior_history_injection.reason_for_removal: Compounding feedback loop at sample sizes too small to be meaningful (1-3 trades per runner). Early luck amplified into overconfidence.
prior_history_injection.impact_of_removal: Positive — removes noise feedback. Prior history at this scale is noise, not signal.

recommendation: THREE changes: (1) MOVE market price to END of prompt after research findings — reduces anchoring in low-research cases; (2) ADD research quality gate (>=2 non-junk sources) before signal generated — prevents extreme outputs; (3) ADD minimum claude_prob divergence check: if |claude_prob - market_prob| < 5pp, suppress signal as 'no view' — Wang would generate phantom edge on these regardless of true value.
summary: Market price IS shown to Claude before research findings (anchoring risk confirmed). Claude is NOT systematically anchoring (avg diff 18.5pp). But 12% of signals show <5pp diff from market, and Wang Transform inflates any anchored signal by 3-7pp — creating 17 confirmed phantom edges in history. More dangerous: when Tavily returns junk sources, Claude generates extreme probabilities (5%/95%) rather than staying near market. Three fixes needed: move price after research in prompt, add research quality gate, suppress near-market signals.
